name: Q2792161
abstract: Freddie Moore (August 20, 1900 – November 3, 1992) was an American jazz drummer, washboarder and singer. Over the span of his 80-year career, he performed in bands with prominent jazz musicians in New York and other cities across the United States, also touring Europe.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Carew
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Freddie Moore only in the context of the etymology of the song title 'Froggy Moore' and speculates that Jelly Roll Morton might have been kidding him. There is no evidence of personal interaction, meeting, or professional collaboration between Roy Carew and Freddie Moore.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Simeon, myself, Don Kirkpatrick, Freddie Moore, we all sit there and listened. And they went through the whole thing. 'Well, you left Mama when we needed you. We was on the road, and you left us and went to school.'

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: The village vanguard used to have nice groups like Art Hodes had a trio there with Max Kaminsky and Freddie Moore.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I got hold of Sidney Bechet and Sidney D. Paris and Freddie Moore, the drummer known as Gate Mouth Moore.

about_quotes.evidence: I got hold of Sidney Bechet and Sidney D. Paris and Freddie Moore, the drummer known as Gate Mouth Moore.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonard Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: the one King had when he had Freddie Moore and Paul Barnes and Clyde Bernhardt.
